title: Peripheral Blood Mononuclear Cells Preparation
author: John Davis Coakley
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6zyhf7w

steps.title: Dilute whole blood 1:1 with 1% FCS HBSS Medium
steps.instruction: Dilute whole blood 1:1 with 1% FCS HBSS Medium

steps.title: Layer this diluted blood onto 10mls Lymphoprep TM
steps.instruction: Layer this diluted blood onto 10mls Lymphoprep TM

steps.title: Centrifuge for 25 minutes at 400G...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 25 minutes at 400G with acceleration and brake off

steps.title: Discard the plasma and obtain the buffy coat
steps.instruction: Discard the plasma and obtain the buffy coat

steps.title: Top up buffy coat with 1%...
steps.instruction: Top up buffy coat with 1% FCS, vortex and Centrifuge for 8 minutes at 1500RPM with brake on. Discard supernatant

steps.title: Top up with 1% FCS, vortex...
steps.instruction: Top up with 1% FCS, vortex and centrifuge for 8 minutes at 1500RPM with brake on. Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Resuspend in 4-5mls of RPMI, and vortex
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 4-5mls of RPMI, and vortex.

steps.title: Count live cells with haemocytometer slide...
steps.instruction: Count live cells with haemocytometer slide after staining with ethidium bromide and acridine orange solution to detect dead and live cells, respectively
